channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Federal student loans will move to Treasury, further shrinking Education Department
item.description: The Trump administration announced a three-phase transition that will eventually include management of most federal student loans as well as the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA).
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration announced a three-phase transition that will eventually include management of most federal student loans as well as the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA).

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: How an act of civil disobedience in 1978 Denver helped propel disability rights
item.description: On July 5-6, 1978, on a busy downtown Denver street, 19 people in wheelchairs blocked public buses--which didn't have wheelchair lifts--to demand access to public transit.
item.content:encoded: On July 5-6, 1978, on a busy downtown Denver street, 19 people in wheelchairs blocked public buses--which didn't have wheelchair lifts--to demand access to public transit.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Shapiro

item.title: Wartime compounds the challenges of reporting on the people of Iran
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks to Reporter and Senior Editor Arezou Rezvani about the impact the war is having on Iranians and people in the surrounding area.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks to Reporter and Senior Editor Arezou Rezvani about the impact the war is having on Iranians and people in the surrounding area.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly

item.title: What levers can the U.S. pull to control gas prices?
item.description: The global oil trade is remarkably flexible. But key solutions that should be able to address the current oil crisis – like reserves, alternate routes, and boosts in production — are constrained.
item.content:encoded: The global oil trade is remarkably flexible. But key solutions that should be able to address the current oil crisis – like reserves, alternate routes, and boosts in production — are constrained.
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: As the Iran war continues, what are the potential off-ramps for Trump?
item.description: The Trump administration says the Iran war will end when the president decides. Thomas Wright of the Brookings Institution argues that's easier said than done.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration says the Iran war will end when the president decides. Thomas Wright of the Brookings Institution argues that's easier said than done.
item.dc:creator: Ailsa Chang

item.title: FDA backs off stricter tanning bed rules with RFK Jr.'s support
item.description: Health officials with the Trump administration have backed away from an effort to more heavily regulate indoor tanning — despite protests from medical groups that warn of the dangers of skin cancer.
item.content:encoded:
Health officials with the Trump administration have backed away from an effort to more heavily regulate indoor tanning — despite protests from medical groups that warn of the dangers of skin cancer.

(Image credit: Adventure_Photo/E+)
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: Researchers find evidence of ancient democracies from the Americas to Europe and Asia
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with archaeologist Gary Feinman about new findings that show democracy existed throughout the ancient world and was not exclusive to Mediterranean Europe.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with archaeologist Gary Feinman about new findings that show democracy existed throughout the ancient world and was not exclusive to Mediterranean Europe.
item.dc:creator: Ailsa Chang

item.title: This week's Short Wave news roundup
item.description: NPR's Short Wave team talks about a wildflower's ability to adapt to climate change, the grooming habits of birds, and the social lives of sharks.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Short Wave team talks about a wildflower's ability to adapt to climate change, the grooming habits of birds, and the social lives of sharks.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Reports say President Trump has damaged democracy at remarkable speed
item.description: Two leading democracy watchdog organizations are releasing studies this week that evaluate the state of American democracy after President Trump's first year.
item.content:encoded: Two leading democracy watchdog organizations are releasing studies this week that evaluate the state of American democracy after President Trump's first year.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: A strike by therapists spotlights a growing concern: AI replacing human providers
item.description: This week, more than 2,000 mental health care workers at Kaiser Permanente, in northern California went on a 24-hour-strike. A major reason is disagreement between Kaiser and therapists over the future role of AI in mental health care.
item.content:encoded: This week, more than 2,000 mental health care workers at Kaiser Permanente, in northern California went on a 24-hour-strike. A major reason is disagreement between Kaiser and therapists over the future role of AI in mental health care.
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee
